audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for pool healing, Sabbath controversy, Father/Son work, life/judgment, witnesses, Scripture/Moses testimony.

verses.restored: After these things there was a feast of the Judeans, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
In Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate there is a pool called in Hebrew Bethesda, having five porticoes.
In these lay a multitude of those who were weak, blind, lame, and withered.
[Some later manuscripts add an angel/messenger stirring the water and a healing tradition about entering first.]
A certain human was there who had been in weakness thirty-eight years.
Jesus, seeing this one lying there and knowing that he had already been so a long time, says to him, Do you want to become whole?
The weakened one answered him, Lord, I have no human to put me into the pool when the water is stirred; while I am coming, another goes down before me.
Jesus says to him, Rise, take up your mat, and walk.
Immediately the human became whole, took up his mat, and walked. Now that day was Sabbath.
Therefore the Judeans were saying to the one healed, It is Sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to carry the mat.
He answered them, The one who made me whole, that one said to me, Take up your mat and walk.
They asked him, Who is the human who said to you, Take up and walk?
The one healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a crowd being in the place.
After these things Jesus finds him in the temple and said to him, Look, you have become whole; do not continue in misalignment, so that something worse may not happen to you.
The human went away and reported to the Judeans that Jesus was the one who made him whole.
Because of this the Judeans were pursuing Jesus, because he was doing these things on Sabbath.
But Jesus answered them, My Father is working until now, and I am working.

Because of this therefore the Judeans were seeking even more to kill him, because not only was he loosening the Sabbath, but he was also calling the Divine his own Father, making himself equal with the Divine.

Jesus answered and said to them, Amen, amen, I say to you: the Son is not able to do anything from himself except what he sees the Father doing; for whatever that One does, these things the Son likewise does.
For the Father loves the Son and shows him all things that he himself does; and greater works than these he will show him, so that you may marvel.
For just as the Father raises the dead and makes alive, so also the Son makes alive whom he wills.
For the Father judges no one, but has given all judgment to the Son,
so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. The one not honoring the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
Amen, amen, I say to you: the one hearing my word and trusting the One who sent me has age-long life and does not come into judgment, but has crossed over from death into life.
Amen, amen, I say to you: an hour is coming and now is, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of the Divine, and those hearing will live.
For just as the Father has life in himself, so he gave also to the Son to have life in himself.
And he gave him authority to make judgment, because he is Son of Humanity.
Do not marvel at this, because an hour is coming in which all those in the tombs will hear his voice,
and will come out: those doing good into resurrection of life, but those practicing harmful things into resurrection of judgment.
I am not able to do anything from myself. As I hear, I judge; and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will but the will of the One who sent me.
If I bear witness concerning myself, my witness is not true/valid by itself.
There is another bearing witness concerning me, and I know that the witness he bears concerning me is true.
You have sent to John, and he has borne witness to the truth.
But I do not receive witness from a human; rather I say these things so that you may be saved/healed.
That one was the lamp burning and shining, and you were willing to rejoice for an hour in his light.
But I have the witness greater than John: the works the Father has given me to complete, these works that I do bear witness concerning me that the Father has sent me.
The Father who sent me has himself borne witness concerning me. You have neither heard his voice at any time nor seen his form,
and you do not have his word remaining in you, because the one whom he sent, this one you do not trust.
You search the Scriptures because you think in them to have age-long life; and those are the ones bearing witness concerning me.
Yet you are not willing to come to me so that you may have life.
I do not receive glory from humans.
But I know you, that you do not have the love of the Divine in yourselves.
I have come in my Father's name, and you do not receive me; if another comes in his own name, that one you will receive.
How are you able to trust, receiving glory from one another and not seeking the glory that is from the only Divine?
Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; the one accusing you is Moses, in whom you have hoped.
For if you trusted Moses, you would trust me, for that one wrote concerning me.
But if you do not trust his writings, how will you trust my words?
